blocks.body: A polynomial with exactly one term. Examples: 5, 3x, -2x^2y, 7ab^3.
To multiply monomials: multiply the coefficients, then multiply the variables using the product rule for exponents. Example: (4x^2y)(3xy^3) = 12x^3y^4.
To divide monomials: divide the coefficients, then divide variables using the quotient rule. Example: 12x^5y^3 / 4x^2y = 3x^3y^2.
When the exponent result is negative, rewrite using the negative exponent rule: x^(-n) = 1/x^n. Example: x^2 / x^5 = x^(-3) = 1/x^3.
